dict.string:
Self-Improving Log — the "self-improving skill" loop (clawhub / Hermes style), as a Shortcut.
THE IDEA: those popular self-improving agent skills don't rewrite themselves — they CAPTURE learnings, mistakes, and corrections to a persistent file and review them later. This shortcut does exactly that on your phone/watch: invoke it from Siri or the Share Sheet, say what you learned, and it appends a timestamped line to a running "Learnings" note you review weekly. Capture → review → promote, by hand, with zero network.
HONEST BOUNDARY: a Shortcut cannot rewrite its own actions or "learn" autonomously — that's an agent's job. This is the capture tool that feeds your improvement loop, which is precisely what the clawhub/Hermes skills are too.
SETUP: point the "Append to Note" action at a note called "Learnings" (or your file of choice). Add it to Siri ("log a learning") and the Share Sheet for capture-anywhere.
What did you learn? (a mistake, a fix, a better way)
